title: Germany National (Long-Stay, Category D) Visa Application
description: Give the details for a German national (long-stay, category D) visa application: the entry visa a non-EU/EEA/Swiss national needs before travelling to Germany to take up employment (including a Blue Card (EU) or Opportunity Card pathway), study, undertake vocational training, or join family already resident in Germany, where the intended stay exceeds the 90-day Schengen short-stay limit. This document models the Federal Foreign Office's (Auswartiges Amt) own bilingual German/English 'Antrag auf Erteilung eines nationalen Visums' / 'Application for a national visa' form, used uniformly across every German mission worldwide regardless of purpose, plus the Consular Services Portal's (digital.diplo.de) pre-login country/mission routing step. The Consular Services Portal has digitised this same paper form for 28 long-stay purposes since January 2025 (Schengen/short-stay visas are explicitly not yet accepted on the portal), but gates the actual data-entry screens behind account registration and sign-in; this document therefore models the form's own field set rather than the authenticated portal UI (see VERIFICATION.md). It does not model a purpose-specific supplementary question set (e.g. Blue Card (EU) salary/qualification thresholds, an employment contract's specifics, or a university admission letter's details) that the portal or mission collects in addition to this base form for a given purpose; each is a candidate for its own future schema or a MINOR-version extension. It does not submit the application; the live source is always authoritative.

source.documentRef: Antrag auf Erteilung eines nationalen Visums / Application for a national visa (bilingual German-English national visa application form, hosted directly by the Federal Foreign Office and mirrored, byte-for-byte identical in content, at every German mission's own site)

verification.notes: Closes the Visa vertical's DE gap (GOV-815 research cycle; Visa was 7/9 after GOV-803 authored ie/irishimmigration/short-stay-visa-avats, with SG and DE remaining). The Consular Services Portal (digital.diplo.de) was driven live with a headless Playwright browser: its public pre-login pages (country-of-residence selection, live-confirmed responsible-mission routing for India across five missions, and the top-level purpose menu — Blue Card (EU), Opportunity Card, Family reunification, Taking up employment, Studies, Vocational training) were reached and screenshotted with no access block, but selecting a country and mission and clicking 'Apply online now' redirects through sso.digital.diplo.de (a Keycloak realm named AUSLPORTAL) and returns 'silent-check-sso.html#error=login_required' — the portal's actual data-entry screens are gated behind Bund ID/account registration, the same login-gated-form class already recorded elsewhere in this registry (e.g. nz/dia/realme-verified-identity). Per source-of-truth fidelity, this document was instead authored field-by-field from the Federal Foreign Office's own canonical PDF, which the portal's own overview page confirms it is digitising 1:1 ('you can apply online... register... submit details plus uploaded documents'). Status remains 'draft' pending a field-by-field pass against the authenticated portal itself. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: countryOfResidence
fields.label: In which country are you resident?
fields.description: Selected from the Consular Services Portal's searchable country list before any account is created. This determines which German mission (embassy/consulate-general) is responsible for the application and is not itself a field on the paper national-visa application form.
fields.sourceRef: Consular Services Portal — live pre-login routing step (digital.diplo.de)

fields.name: responsibleMission
fields.label: Your responsible German mission
fields.description: The specific German mission (e.g. a named embassy or consulate-general) the Consular Services Portal determines is responsible for the applicant, based on countryOfResidence. Confirmed live for India: Bangalore, Chennai, Kolkata, Mumbai, or New Delhi, each covering a distinct set of states/union territories. Selecting a country with only one mission skips this choice.
fields.sourceRef: Consular Services Portal — live pre-login routing step (digital.diplo.de)

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital status
fields.description: The form's German original lists ledig (single), verheiratet seit (married since), eingetragene Lebenspartnerschaft seit (civil union since), geschieden seit (divorced since), verwitwet seit (widowed since); the '...since' qualifier is captured separately in maritalStatusSinceDate.

fields.name: maritalStatusSinceDate
fields.label: Date since when the current marital status applies

fields.name: travelDocumentType
fields.label: Type of travel document (exact designation)
fields.description: 'Special passport' corresponds to the form's 'sonstiger amtlicher Pass (Servicepass)'.

fields.name: residencePermitNumber
fields.label: Residence permit or equivalent document no.

fields.name: childrenCount
fields.label: Number of children (including children over 18)
fields.description: Section 3 requires information on the applicant's children, including those aged over 18, even if they remain abroad.

fields.name: childrenDetails
fields.label: Children's details (surname, first names, sex, date and place of birth, nationality, place of residence)
fields.description: One row per child on the source form's table. This document collapses the repeating table into a single free-text field, the same treatment used elsewhere in this registry for small repeating tables (e.g. ie/irishimmigration/short-stay-visa-avats' dependentChildrenDetails).

fields.name: fatherSurname
fields.label: Father's surname
fields.description: Section 4 states this information is also required if the person concerned remains abroad.

fields.name: fatherDateAndPlaceOfBirth
fields.label: Father's date and place of birth
fields.description: Modelled as a single field because the source form combines date and place of birth into one table column for this section (unlike Section 1, which uses separate boxes for the applicant).

fields.name: motherSurname
fields.label: Mother's surname
fields.description: Section 4 states this information is also required if the person concerned remains abroad.

fields.name: motherDateAndPlaceOfBirth
fields.label: Mother's date and place of birth
fields.description: Modelled as a single field because the source form combines date and place of birth into one table column for this section (unlike Section 1, which uses separate boxes for the applicant).

fields.name: previouslyInGermany
fields.label: Have you ever been to Germany before?

fields.name: previousStaysDetails
fields.label: Dates and places of your last five stays in Germany (month/year)
fields.description: The source form provides five from/to/place rows; collapsed into one free-text field, the same treatment used for other small repeating tables in this document.

fields.name: intendedStayStreet
fields.label: Intended place of stay in Germany — street, number (if known)

fields.name: intendedStayPostcodePlace
fields.label: Intended place of stay in Germany — postcode, place

fields.name: apartmentRoomsCount
fields.label: Number of rooms in the apartment

fields.name: maintainsPermanentResidenceOutsideGermany
fields.label: Do you intend to maintain your permanent residence outside the Federal Republic of Germany?

fields.name: familyMembersAccompanying
fields.label: Do family members intend to accompany you?

fields.name: purposeOfStay
fields.label: Purpose of stay in the Federal Republic of Germany
fields.description: The Consular Services Portal (digital.diplo.de) organises this same underlying purpose into 28 more granular online application categories, e.g. three Blue Card (EU) variants and an Opportunity Card under 'Employment', and separate 'Taking up employment', 'Family reunification', 'Studies', and 'Vocational training' menu entries — confirmed live for Employment/Blue Card/Opportunity Card/Family reunification/Studies/Vocational training. This document models the base national-visa application data common to every purpose; a purpose-specific supplementary question set (e.g. Blue Card salary/qualification thresholds) is out of scope. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: stayNotExceedingTwelveMonths
fields.label: I intend to stay no longer than twelve months in the Federal territory and apply for a visa covering the whole duration of my stay
fields.description: The exact statement the applicant confirms on the source form.

fields.name: referenceStreet
fields.label: Reference in Germany — street, number
fields.description: E.g. employer, educational establishment, or relatives in Germany. The source form does not mark this section as universally mandatory.

fields.name: professionTrained
fields.label: Trade or profession for which you trained

fields.name: currentProfessionIfDifferent
fields.label: Current trade or profession, if different from the one you trained for

fields.name: intendedDurationFrom
fields.label: Intended duration of stay in Germany — from

fields.name: intendedDurationTo
fields.label: Intended duration of stay in Germany — to

fields.name: meansOfSubsistence
fields.label: What are your means of subsistence?

fields.name: hasHealthInsuranceForGermany
fields.label: Do you have health insurance that covers the Federal Republic of Germany?

fields.name: convictionGermanyWhenWhere
fields.label: Conviction germany — when and where
fields.description: Neither the Germany nor the Abroad sub-block is guaranteed to apply; the applicant completes whichever is relevant.

fields.name: convictionGermanyReason
fields.label: Conviction germany — reason (what was the crime)

fields.name: convictionGermanyNatureAndExtent
fields.label: Conviction germany — nature and extent of penalty

fields.name: convictionAbroadWhenWhere
fields.label: Conviction abroad — when and where
fields.description: Neither the Germany nor the Abroad sub-block is guaranteed to apply; the applicant completes whichever is relevant.

fields.name: convictionAbroadReason
fields.label: Conviction abroad — reason (what was the crime)

fields.name: convictionAbroadNatureAndExtent
fields.label: Conviction abroad — nature and extent of penalty

fields.name: previouslyExpelledDeportedOrRefused
fields.label: Have you ever been expelled or deported from the Federal Republic of Germany, had an application for a residence permit rejected, or been refused entry into the Federal Republic of Germany?

fields.name: suffersNotifiableDisease
fields.label: Do you suffer from (or are you suspected of suffering from) any of the diseases listed in footnote 1?
fields.description: Footnote 1 lists: smallpox, poliomyelitis, types of influenza not common in humans (e.g. bird flu, swine flu), acute pandemic influenza, severe acute respiratory syndrome (SARS), cholera, pneumonic plague, yellow fever, and viral haemorrhagic fever (e.g. Ebola, Lassa, Marburg).
fields.sourceRef: National visa application form — Section 16, footnote 1

documents.label: Passport-style photo
documents.sourceRef: National visa application form — page 1 header: 'Photo. Please do not use glue to attach the photo.'

documents.label: Signed declaration that the application's particulars are correct and complete
documents.statement: Ich versichere, vorstehende Angaben nach bestem Wissen und Gewissen richtig und vollstandig gemacht zu haben. / I declare that to the best of my knowledge the above particulars are correct and complete.
documents.sourceRef: National visa application form — page 4, exact bilingual attestation text, signed and dated (place and date) by the applicant, or by a minor's parent/legal guardian)

documents.label: Signed acknowledgement of the legal consequences of false or incomplete information (Section 54(2) No. 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act)
documents.statement: Ein Auslander kann ausgewiesen werden, wenn er falsche oder unvollstandige Angaben zur Erlangung eines deutschen Aufenthaltstitels oder eines Schengen-Visums macht... / A foreigner can be expelled if he or she has furnished false or incomplete information in order to gain a German residence permit or a Schengen visa. The applicant is obliged to provide all information to the best of his or her knowledge and belief. If he or she refuses to provide data or knowingly furnishes false or incomplete information, the visa application may be refused or, if a visa has already been issued, the applicant may be expelled from Germany.
documents.sourceRef: National visa application form — page 5, 'Belehrung nach Section 54 Abs. 2 Nr. 8 i.V.m. Section 53 AufenthG' / 'Information provided to applicants pursuant to Section 54 (2) 8 in conjunction with Section 53 of the Residence Act', signed and dated separately from the main declaration

steps.title: Country of residence and responsible mission
Information on the applicant — travel document and residence permit
Information on the applicant's spouse/registered partner
Intended place of stay in Germany
Purpose of stay in the Federal Republic of Germany
References in the Federal Republic of Germany
Means of subsistence and health insurance
